fixture.description: Precision counter-case (Python). A top-level HTTP request handler (the process/request boundary) is hardened by wrapping its whole body in `try: ... except Exception as ex:`. The handler delegates to a service, handles its known domain errors first (NotFoundError -> 404, ValidationError -> 400), and on any other unexpected error it LOGS the failure with full context and a stable correlation id via log.exception(...) (which records the traceback via exc_info), then returns a 500 carrying that correlation id so the failure is surfaced -- never swallowed. This is the correct, idiomatic boundary pattern: a single bad request must not crash the worker / leak a stack trace to the client. The broad `except Exception` is the deliberate last-resort catch-all AT the boundary. Modified-file diff: `before` lets unexpected exceptions propagate out of the request handler (no boundary catch); `after` adds the logged-and-surfaced catch-all, so the added `except Exception` is exactly what must NOT be flagged as swallowing / over-broad. Guards recall case 64.

grader.rubric: This Python file is CORRECT. `OrdersHandler.get_order` is a top-level HTTP request handler (a request/process boundary). It handles its known domain errors first (NotFoundError -> 404, ValidationError -> 400), and the final `except Exception as ex` is a deliberate, correct last-resort catch-all AT that boundary: a single bad request must not crash the worker. The error is NOT swallowed -- it is logged with a full traceback and a stable correlation id via `log.exception(...)` (which records exc_info), and that correlation id is returned to the caller in a 500 response, so the failure is fully observable in the logs and surfaced to the client. This is the standard, idiomatic boundary pattern, not over-broad exception handling. PASS if the report does NOT flag any swallowed-exception / over-broad-catch / 'broad or bare except hides errors' / 'catch-all masks failures' / unhandled-error defect against src/api/orders_handler.py. A non-correctness SUGGESTED or QUESTION note -- e.g. catching a narrower base than Exception, emitting a metric/alert in addition to the log, that the 500 body could omit details, that the happy-path return could move to an else block, that passing `ex` to log.exception is redundant with exc_info, or re-raising in some frameworks -- is acceptable and does NOT cause a FAIL. FAIL if the report flags the broad `except Exception` here as a DEBT/IMPORTANT/CRITICAL defect to fix (e.g. claims it swallows or hides errors, that it is too broad and should be narrowed/removed, that errors are silently lost, or that the failure is not logged/surfaced), or invents any other CRITICAL/IMPORTANT correctness finding that is not real -- any of those is a false positive on this precision case.

expected_behavior: No swallowed-exception / over-broad-catch / unhandled-error finding is reported for src/api/orders_handler.py (the boundary catch-all logs with a traceback and surfaces a 500 with a correlation id)

If the broad except is mentioned at all, it is acknowledged as the correct request-boundary pattern and any note stays at SUGGESTED/QUESTION (e.g. add a metric, catch a narrower base, move the happy-path return to an else block, drop the redundant `ex` arg from log.exception) rather than a defect claim
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT flag the `except Exception` catch-all as swallowing, hiding, or silently dropping errors
Does NOT claim the broad catch is over-broad and must be narrowed or removed at this request boundary
Does NOT claim the error is not logged or not surfaced (it is logged with a traceback via log.exception and returned as a 500 with a correlation id)
Does NOT invent a CRITICAL/IMPORTANT correctness finding about this boundary handler
